title: spathulata spuria
intro: Spathulata spuria, also known as the 'Bullrush', is a type of aquatic plant native to the wetlands of the Americas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Spathulata spuria include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery. Spathulata spuria is typically prepared for medicinal use by drying the roots and leaves, then making them into teas, infusions, or decoctions, or using them in poultices and salves.
